county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Dickinson County formed 1857 from an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Abilene. Named for Daniel S. Dickinson, US Senator from New York. Abilene was the most famous end-of-trail cattle town (1867-1871). Birthplace and boyhood home of President Dwight D. Eisenhower. Mennonite and German-Russian settlement area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Dickinson County Register of Deeds in Abilene holds land records from 1857. Abilene was the famous end-of-trail cattle town (1867-1871) -- town marshal records and cattle trade documents at KSHS. The Dickinson County Historical Society in Abilene has excellent local collections. The Eisenhower Presidential Library in Abilene holds Eisenhower family records and some local history. BLM GLO land patents. Strong Mennonite (River Brethren) and German-Russian communities left church records from the 1870s -- check Bethel College archives. Probate Court records from 1857. Abilene newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Dickinson County records. The Kansas Pacific Railroad brought settlers; check railroad land sale records at KSHS.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Dickinson County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Abilene

source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival from 1857; deed books are substantially complete

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Doniphan County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Troy. Named for Colonel Alexander W. Doniphan of Missouri. One of the earliest-settled Kansas counties along the Missouri River. Iowa, Sac & Fox, and Kickapoo reservation lands partially within the county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Doniphan County Register of Deeds in Troy holds land records from 1855. One of the earliest- settled Kansas counties along the Missouri River. The Iowa and Sac & Fox Kickapoo reservations overlap parts of the county -- tribal records at NARA Kansas City. The Doniphan County Historical Society in Troy has local collections including early settler files. BLM GLO land patents. German and Irish immigrant communities produced church records from the 1850s. Probate Court records from 1855. Troy and White Cloud newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Doniphan County records. Some of the earliest Kansas territorial-era records survive for this county at KSHS.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Doniphan County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Troy

county_metadata.record_losses: Sacking of Lawrence (1856) and Quantrill's Raid (1863) caused destruction of early records and town documents
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Douglas County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Lawrence. Named for Stephen A. Douglas, author of the Kansas-Nebraska Act. Epicenter of Bleeding Kansas conflict. Lawrence was sacked in 1856 and destroyed by Quantrill's Raid in 1863, with nearly 200 men killed. Home of the University of Kansas.
county_metadata.research_tips: Douglas County suffered major record losses from the Sacking of Lawrence (1856) and Quantrill's Raid (August 21, 1863), which killed nearly 200 men and destroyed much of the town. Register of Deeds in Lawrence has post-raid records. The Kansas Collection at the Kenneth Spencer Research Library, University of Kansas, is an essential supplementary resource with extensive territorial-era manuscripts, Free State movement papers, and local family collections. The Watkins Museum of History (Douglas County Historical Society) in Lawrence has local genealogical files. BLM GLO land patents fill land record gaps. KSHS holds territorial records including Free State legislature documents. Haskell Indian Nations University records document Native American students. FamilySearch has microfilmed surviving Douglas County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Douglas County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Lawrence

source_profiles.source_name: Douglas County Land Records (post-loss) -- Register of Deeds / BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Douglas County Register of Deeds, Lawrence / BLM GLO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County land records with gaps due to Sacking of Lawrence (1856) and Quantrill's Raid (1863) caused destruction of early records and town documents. BLM GLO federal land patents supplement destroyed county deed books. Register of Deeds holds surviving and post-loss records
source_profiles.survival_notes: Incomplete: Sacking of Lawrence (1856) and Quantrill's Raid (1863) caused destruction of early records and town documents. BLM GLO patents provide partial backup for federal land entries

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Edwards County formed 1874 from Kiowa County. County seat is Kinsley. Named for W.C. Edwards, early settler. Kinsley claims the midpoint between New York and San Francisco. Santa Fe Trail corridor. Fort Larned (nearby) was a significant military post.
county_metadata.research_tips: Edwards County Register of Deeds in Kinsley holds land records from 1874. Kinsley is the midpoint between New York City and San Francisco on the Santa Fe Trail/railroad. The Edwards County Historical Society and Museum in Kinsley has local collections. Formed from Kiowa County -- check predecessor records. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. Probate Court records from 1874. German-Russian settlers left Catholic and Lutheran church records. Kinsley newspapers at KSHS from the 1870s. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Edwards County records. Fort Larned (nearby) military records at NARA document early settlers and military families in the area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Edwards County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Kinsley

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Elk County formed 1875 from the abolished Howard County. County seat is Howard. Named for the Elk River. Located in the Flint Hills region. Ranching economy. Small, declining population since the early 1900s.
county_metadata.research_tips: Elk County Register of Deeds in Howard holds land records from 1875. Formed from the abolished Howard County -- check Howard County records at KSHS for pre-1875 entries. The Elk County Historical Society has local collections and cemetery transcriptions. BLM GLO land patents. The county is in the Flint Hills with ranching as the primary economy. Probate Court records from 1875. Methodist and Baptist church records from pioneer congregations. Howard newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. The county's small population means fewer records overall; check neighboring Chautauqua and Greenwood county records for cross-border families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Elk County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Howard

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Ellis County formed 1867, organized 1867. County seat is Hays. Named for Lieutenant George Ellis, killed in Civil War. Fort Hays (1865-1889) was a major military post. One of the largest Volga German (German-Russian) communities in North America. Fort Hays State University. Cathedral of the Plains (St. Fidelis) in Victoria.
county_metadata.research_tips: Ellis County Register of Deeds in Hays holds land records from the 1870s. Hays was a major railroad and military town (Fort Hays, 1865-1889). Fort Hays State University's Forsyth Library Special Collections hold extensive Volga German (German-Russian) research materials -- the Ellis County area has one of the largest Volga German communities in North America. Catholic parish records from St. Fidelis Cathedral ('Cathedral of the Plains') in Victoria and other parishes are critical. The Ellis County Historical Society and Museum has local collections. BLM GLO land patents. NARA holds Fort Hays military records. Probate Court records from the 1870s. Hays newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Ellis County records. The Volga German Society maintains genealogical databases.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Ellis County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Hays

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Ellsworth County formed 1867. County seat is Ellsworth. Named for Fort Ellsworth (later Fort Harker). Cattle town on the Kansas Pacific Railroad (1871-1875). German-Russian settlers arrived in the 1870s. Kanopolis Lake area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Ellsworth County Register of Deeds in Ellsworth holds land records from 1867. Ellsworth was a cattle town on the Kansas Pacific Railroad (1871-1875). The Hodgden House Museum and Ellsworth County Historical Society has local collections. BLM GLO land patents. German-Russian settlers arrived in the 1870s; check Catholic and Lutheran church records. Probate Court records from 1867. Ellsworth Reporter newspaper at KSHS. Fort Harker (nearby) military records at NARA document early settlers. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Ellsworth County records. Kanopolis Lake construction displaced some communities; check KSHS for affected family records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Ellsworth County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Ellsworth

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Finney County formed 1884 from Ford and other counties. County seat is Garden City. Named for Lieutenant David W. Finney, killed in Indian conflict. Major meatpacking and irrigated agriculture center. Diverse modern population including Mexican American, Southeast Asian, and Somali communities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Finney County Register of Deeds in Garden City holds land records from 1884. Garden City became a major meatpacking center -- Tyson and other plant records may document workers. The Finney County Historical Society and Museum in Garden City has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries in this western Kansas county. Significant Mexican American and Southeast Asian immigrant communities from the late 20th century. Early German-Russian and Mennonite settlers left church records. Probate Court records from 1884. Garden City Telegram newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Finney County records. The county includes former Arapaho and Sequoyah county territories.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Finney County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Garden City

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Ford County formed 1873. County seat is Dodge City. Named for Colonel James H. Ford, Civil War officer. Dodge City was the most famous cattle town in the American West. Fort Dodge military post (1865-1882). Boot Hill cemetery. Santa Fe Trail crossing.
county_metadata.research_tips: Ford County Register of Deeds in Dodge City holds land records from the 1870s. Dodge City was the most famous cattle town in Kansas -- the Wyatt Earp, Bat Masterson era is extensively documented at the Boot Hill Museum and KSHS. Town marshal records, saloon licenses, and cattle shipping records at KSHS. The Ford County Historical Society has local collections. BLM GLO land patents. Fort Dodge military records (1865-1882) at NARA document soldiers and families. German-Russian Mennonite settlers arrived in the 1870s-1880s; check Mennonite church records and Bethel College archives. Probate Court records from the 1870s. Dodge City newspapers at KSHS and Newspapers.com. FamilySearch has microfilmed Ford County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Ford County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Dodge City

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Franklin County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Ottawa. Named for Benjamin Franklin. Border war violence (1856-1858) affected eastern portions. Ottawa University (Baptist). Free State stronghold. Marais des Cygnes River valley.
county_metadata.research_tips: Franklin County suffered record losses from border war violence (1856-1858) in eastern portions near the Missouri border. Register of Deeds in Ottawa holds surviving and post-war records. The Franklin County Historical Society in Ottawa has local genealogical collections. Ottawa University archives hold local church records. BLM GLO land patents fill gaps in destroyed land records. KSHS holds territorial-era documents for Franklin County. Probate Court records may have gaps in the earliest years. Ottawa newspapers at KSHS from the 1860s. FamilySearch has microfilmed Franklin County records. The county had strong Free State connections; check KSHS manuscript collections for abolitionist records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Franklin County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Ottawa

source_profiles.source_name: Franklin County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Franklin County Register of Deeds, Ottawa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Franklin County from 1855; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages; liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival from 1855; deed books are substantially complete

source_profiles.source_name: Franklin County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Franklin County Probate Court, Ottawa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Franklin County; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters

source_profiles.source_name: Franklin County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Franklin County; Kansas Territory census 1855 and 1859 may include Franklin County entries at KSHS; state census 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905, 1915, 1925 at KSHS

source_profiles.source_name: Franklin County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (homestead, preemption, timber culture, cash entry) for Franklin County; Kansas is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Franklin County Territorial-Era Records -- KSHS
source_profiles.repository: Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kansas territorial records for Franklin County including territorial census (1855, 1859), land claims, Free State and pro-slavery legislature records, correspondence, and early settler petitions held at KSHS

source_profiles.repository: Franklin County Probate Court, Ottawa / Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records for Franklin County from 1855; Probate Court issued marriage licenses in Kansas; KSHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Kansas marriages; state vital records registration began 1911

source_profiles.source_name: Franklin County Naturalization Records -- District Court
source_profiles.repository: Franklin County District Court, Ottawa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention and petitions for naturalization filed in Franklin County District Court; document immigrant arrivals and origins; held at the courthouse or transferred to KSHS
